creator: mat@FreeBSD.org
text:
+--On 15 avril 2014 11:42:36 +0200 "O. Hartmann"
<ohartman@zedat.fu-berlin.de> wrote:
| On Tue, 15 Apr 2014 09:20:33 GMT
| mat@FreeBSD.org wrote:
| 
|> Synopsis: dns/bind-tools: bind-tools-9.9.5_10 conflicts with
|> bind99-9.9.5_10 (installs files into the same place)
|> 
|> State-Changed-From-To: open->closed
|> State-Changed-By: mat
|> State-Changed-When: Tue Apr 15 09:19:21 UTC 2014
|> State-Changed-Why: 
|> Of course they install at the same place, one installs a subset of the
|> other.
|> 
|> You can remove bind-tools though, nagios-plugins has not depended on it
|> for a long time.
|> 
|> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=188645
| 
| 
| I deleted all the nagios plugins and nagios itself and removed all
| configs via rmconfig-recursive. But whenever I try to install nagios
| again, dns/bind-tools is getting reeled in.

Trying to have check_dig and check_dns work right is really not that easy.
There was a build time dependency on it from yesterday afternoon to this
morning, but if you had BIND installed the ports infrastructure would have
used that in its stead. (If your ports tree dates from late November last
year, there was a one day window where there was a runtime dependency on
bind-tools too.)
Since this morning, it doesn't try to use BIND by default.

        -- 
Mathieu Arnold
